message:
feat(prune): implement comprehensive skill pruning analysis system

Expands the prune command with intelligent analysis capabilities to identify
skills that are candidates for deprecation, merging, or removal based on
usage patterns, quality metrics, similarity detection, and toolchain relevance.

New subcommands in src/cli/commands/prune.rs:

`prune analyze`:
- Low Usage Detection: Skills with suggest_count < threshold over time window
- Quality Scoring: Skills below quality_score threshold (embeddings, metadata)
- Similarity Detection: High cosine similarity pairs that may be duplicates
- Toolchain Mismatch: Skills targeting obsolete or unavailable tools
- Staleness Detection: Skills not updated within staleness window
- Combined scoring with configurable weights for each factor

Analysis output includes:
- Candidate skills ranked by prune priority score
- Reason breakdown (why each skill is flagged)
- Suggested action (deprecate, merge, archive, delete)
- Impact assessment (dependency count, usage frequency)

`prune proposals`:
- Generate actionable proposals from analysis results
- Deprecate proposals: Mark skill as deprecated with successor
- Merge proposals: Combine similar skills with content preservation
- Archive proposals: Move to archive with audit trail
- Optional beads integration: Create beads issues for proposals
  - Links proposals to issue tracker for team review
  - Supports --create-beads flag for automatic issue creation

Analysis configuration:
- --min-usage: Minimum suggest_count threshold (default: 5)
- --quality-threshold: Minimum quality score (default: 0.3)
- --similarity-threshold: Max similarity before flagging (default: 0.85)
- --staleness-days: Days since update to flag stale (default: 180)
- --toolchain-file: Path to supported toolchain manifest

Scoring algorithm:
- usage_score: normalize(suggest_count, 0, max_usage)
- quality_score: skill.quality_score or embedding-derived score
- freshness_score: 1.0 - (days_since_update / staleness_window)
- relevance_score: toolchain availability check
- prune_priority = weighted_sum(1-usage, 1-quality, 1-freshness, 1-relevance)

Use cases:
- Periodic skill library maintenance and cleanup
- Identify duplicate skills for consolidation
- Find outdated skills targeting deprecated tools
- Generate maintenance backlog items via beads integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
